mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP and retail sales indicate expanding aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical difference from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals labor market stabilization, reducing recession risk premiums and lifting equity valuations within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in over 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production confirms broad-based economic recovery, compressing credit spreads and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint growth signals produce negative or flat SPX returns in more than 40% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: In an inflationary environment, rising CPI combined with elevated federal funds rates forces discount rate expansion, depressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral despite simultaneous CPI and rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in industrial production indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.5 index points in 3 months
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of instances following INDPRO acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects durable labor demand, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window despite consistent payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth outpacing CPI inflation indicates overheating demand, prompting anticipatory monetary tightening that raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite GDP growth significantly exceeding inflation rates.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid federal funds rate hikes signal aggressive monetary tightening, increasing borrowing costs and equity risk premiums, which suppresses S&P 500 valuations and yields negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in 2 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive in over 50% of instances following rapid rate hikes.
